$schema-note: The machine mirror of CODE-STYLE.md — a structured ruleset ('biome config, as a skill'). Each rule declares HOW it is enforced (its channel) and the concrete artifact that enforces it. CODE-STYLE.md stays the human prose; this file is what the grill-me review/coach skills read to enforce every rule deterministically, not just the 'Never' list. SSOT for the machine view; keep the two in step.
gate: verify (biome ci . && tsc --noEmit && vitest run && build)

channels.biome-builtin: A Biome recommended rule already catches it — runs in `biome ci`, zero extra config.
channels.biome-builtin-scoped: A Biome rule enabled for a path subset via biome.json `overrides`.
channels.biome-grit-plugin: A custom GritQL rule in biome-rules/*.grit (call/expression shaped). Autofix via `=>` when marked safe.
channels.judgment: No deterministic detector — GritQL can't express it (e.g. declarations) or it is taste/architecture. Enforced by the review sub-agents + `deslop`/`deslop-v2` per-diff.

rules.statement: unknown, never any in hand-written code.
rules.bad: let b: any = 1; const a = (x as any).y;

rules.statement: Node builtins use the node: prefix.
rules.source: Imports: node: prefix, barrels per directory

rules.statement: Never console.* in the CLI — go through core/ui.ts. Harness scripts may use console.* as their output contract.
rules.artifact: biome.json overrides[src/core/**, src/commands/**].suspicious/noConsole = error (harness intentionally excluded)

rules.statement: A runtime dependency must never be reachable from the hook payload — hooks import only node:*, src/payload/*, and the feature's own lib/.
Never commit a focused test — `.only(` silently skips the rest of the suite.

rules.artifact: linter.rules.style/useFilenamingConvention { filenameCases: [camelCase] } — DOCUMENTED, not yet enabled (audit index.ts / *.test.ts naming before turning on).
GritQL 2.5 cannot match `enum` declarations and `recommended` does not flag them — caught by the review sub-agents + deslop. Revisit as a grit rule when Biome adds declaration matching.

rules.statement: Every exported function/type carries TSDoc: summary + @param each + @returns + one line per property. Internal one-liners stay bare.
rules.artifact: deslop / jsdoc-editor per-diff — not mechanically checkable end-to-end.
rules.source: TSDoc on the exported surface / ADR 0012

rules.statement: A module that touches disk/env/process splits pure transformers (top) from effects (bottom) with a `// --- IO layer ---` divider; tests hit the pure half.
rules.artifact: Architectural — review sub-agents check the divider + that tests exercise the pure half.
rules.bad: function readAndMerge(file) { const s = readFileSync(file); return merge(s); }

rules.statement: Pure mutators take a value and return a new one via structuredClone — never mutate an argument.
rules.artifact: Semantic — review sub-agents (noParameterAssign catches only direct reassignment, not deep mutation).
rules.source: Pure mutators clone in, clone out

rules.statement: Declare a shared contract once, re-export it — never re-declare (config SSOT lives in src/payload/config.ts).
rules.artifact: Cross-module — review sub-agents; a divergent re-declared key is invisible to a single-file linter.
rules.bad: export const ENV_KEYS = { /* re-declared copy */ } as const;
rules.source: Declare a shared contract once / ADR 0003

rules.statement: The installer copies only the catalog-declared `ships` paths — build-only .ts never leaks into a user's install.
rules.artifact: Architectural invariant — review sub-agents + the install/uninstall integration test.
